title: Spring Pea & Asparagus Soup with Goat Cheese Toasts
description: A bright, fresh green soup of peas and asparagus lifted with mint, served with crisp garlic toasts topped with goat cheese and a touch of chilli.

ingredients.name: Garlic clove (to rub on the toasts)

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil. Cook the asparagus for 3-4 minutes until just tender, then lift out with a slotted spoon and plunge into cold water to keep its colour. Cook the peas in the same water for 2-3 minutes, then drain.
Set aside a few whole asparagus tips and peas for garnish. Roughly chop the rest of the asparagus.
Blend the cooked peas, chopped asparagus, mint leaves and vegetable stock until completely smooth. Season with salt and adjust the stock quantity for a thinner or thicker soup.
Warm the soup gently over low heat without boiling (to keep its bright colour), and check the seasoning.

For the toasts: toast the bread slices, then rub one side with the cut side of the garlic clove while still warm. Drizzle with olive oil, spread with goat cheese, and finish with a pinch of Espelette pepper.
Ladle the soup into bowls, garnish with the reserved peas and asparagus tips, and serve with the warm toasts alongside.
footnote: Quantities for the soup and toasts are estimated for 4 starter-sized portions — let me know if you had different amounts in mind and I'll adjust. Keep the soup warm but never let it boil once blended, to preserve its bright green colour.
